topic: Magnesium types ranked: which actually works for sleep
caption:
Struggling to fall asleep despite taking magnesium? 😴 You might be using the wrong type! Not all magnesium supplements are created equal, especially when it comes to crossing the blood-brain barrier to calm your nervous system. We break down the science: only specific forms like Magnesium Glycinate and L-Threonate are truly effective for sleep, directly influencing GABA production and melatonin regulation. Meanwhile, popular types like Citrate and Oxide offer minimal to no sleep benefits, often acting more as laxatives. Understanding the right form can transform your nightly rest. 🧠✨
